title: Endocrine Society 2018 Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism
supports_claim: Symptoms/signs plus consistently low testosterone, repeat morning confirmation, LH/FSH cause workup, and major safety exclusions including fertility planning, prostate/breast cancer, elevated hematocrit, severe untreated OSA, severe LUTS, uncontrolled HF, recent MI/stroke, thrombophilia.
does_not_support: Does not support testosterone as a wellness shortcut without medical eligibility.
evidence_directness: direct for medical eligibility and safety-gate framework

title: AUA Testosterone Deficiency Guideline
supports_claim: Total testosterone below 300 ng/dL is a reasonable cut-off in support of diagnosis when combined with symptoms/signs.
does_not_support: Does not support treating a lab value alone or wellness goals alone.

title: ACP 2020 Testosterone Treatment in Adult Men With Age-Related Low Testosterone
supports_claim: For age-related low testosterone, ACP supports treatment discussion primarily for sexual dysfunction and recommends against initiating testosterone for energy, vitality, physical function, or cognition.
does_not_support: Does not address every organic hypogonadism pathway.

title: FDA 2025 Class-Wide Labeling Changes for Testosterone Products
supports_claim: FDA updated testosterone labeling after TRAVERSE and ABPM studies; added blood-pressure warnings and included CV-safety findings.
does_not_support: Not a reason to ignore BP/CV monitoring or treat testosterone as risk-free.

title: HHS 2026 Requested Updates to Testosterone Therapy Labeling
supports_claim: Requested updates on age-related hypogonadism, prostate cancer risk, and BPH warnings; still recommends prostate-risk assessment, screening, and monitoring; severe BPH data remain limited.
does_not_support: Not a universal clearance for prostate/BPH risk or replacement for clinical guidelines.

title: TRAVERSE Cardiovascular Safety of Testosterone-Replacement Therapy
supports_claim: In men with hypogonadism and preexisting/high CV risk, TRT was noninferior to placebo for major adverse cardiac events.
does_not_support: Does not prove testosterone is risk-free or appropriate for all wellness clients.
evidence_directness: direct CV-safety context in indicated monitored population

title: AUA/ASRM Male Infertility Guideline
supports_claim: Exogenous testosterone can suppress spermatogenesis; fertility intentions require a fertility-preserving pathway review.
does_not_support: Does not support routine exogenous TRT when fertility is desired.

title: ISSN Position Stand: Creatine supplementation and exercise
supports_claim: Creatine monohydrate supports strength/power and lean-mass training adaptations in appropriate users.
does_not_support: Does not replace testosterone eligibility logic.

title: NIH Vitamin D Fact Sheet
supports_claim: Vitamin D supports bone/muscle health; serum 25-OH vitamin D can guide supplementation.
does_not_support: Does not support vitamin D as testosterone replacement.

title: NIH Magnesium Fact Sheet
supports_claim: Magnesium supports neuromuscular and metabolic physiology; renal function matters for safety.
does_not_support: Does not support magnesium as TRT substitute.
